text:
**Dropped the coding-agent anchor.** The lead now talks about "an AI" and "your knowledge" generically. The differentiator — verbatim, cited, no-paraphrase — is about *how knowledge is held*, which has nothing to do with code, so the framing follows the thesis instead of the host. Concrete changes:
- Example in "show your work" is now domain-neutral (primary-sources-vs-blog-posts, not bun/typing).
- The wiki example is "a single subject you've worked out," not "how auth works in this project."
- The Claude Code section is retitled **"The assistant integration"** and explicitly says *"Nothing about capture-then-inject is specific to Claude Code, or to code… Claude Code is simply the host that exposes those seams today."*
- Quick-start now notes the engine "stands on its own, no assistant required."
